nodes.text: 2:13 AM. Your phone screams: 'EMERGENCY ALERT — Stay inside.' Outside, a dog is barking like it’s trying to warn the entire universe. Then your front door handle clicks… slowly.

choices.label: Kill the lights and stay still

nodes.text: You kill the lights. The house becomes a black box. The door handle stops moving—like whatever’s outside is listening for you to breathe wrong.

choices.label: Crawl to your bedroom closet and hide
Text your best friend: 'Something’s at my door.'

nodes.text: You lift the blind two millimeters. On your porch: your neighbor. Barefoot. Face smeared. Their mouth hangs open like they forgot how to be a person.

choices.label: Back away quietly and lock everything

nodes.text: You call 911. It rings. And rings. Then a recorded voice: 'All operators are busy.' In the background, your front door handle jiggles harder.
You try again. This time someone answers… then the line goes dead. Your porch light flickers once. The handle stops. Silence.
You text: 'Something’s outside.' Your friend replies instantly: 'DON’T OPEN ANYTHING. TURN OFF LIGHTS. STAY QUIET.' Helpful. Also terrifying.

choices.label: Go to the kitchen for supplies

nodes.text: You lock the deadbolt. Chain lock. Back door. Sliding glass. You realize you’ve never appreciated a lock this much in your life.

choices.label: Go to the kitchen to arm up
Check your garage for a way out
Go upstairs and look from a safer angle

nodes.text: You whisper. The figure snaps its head toward the sound like a cat hearing a can open. It slams into the door with its shoulder.
You need something. Anything. You’re in pajamas. This is not the fit for dying.

choices.label: Go to the kitchen for a knife
Go to the garage (tools, car, exit)
Grab a flashlight from the hallway drawer

nodes.text: You open the drawer quietly and feel around. Your fingers hit plastic. Flashlight.

choices.label: Go to the kitchen for more

nodes.text: Kitchen. Your safe little normal place—now a weapons menu. You spot a knife block, bottled water, and a first aid kit under the sink.
Knife acquired. You immediately feel 12% safer and 200% more delusional about your chances in a horror scenario.
You grab bottled water. Survival brain kicks in. You hate that it’s working.
First aid kit secured. If you survive tonight, you’re never skipping a CPR class again.
Upstairs. You peek through a second-floor window. Streetlights glow. Two houses down, someone is sprinting in the road… and something else is sprinting faster behind them.

choices.label: Try to help by shouting (bad idea)

nodes.text: You shout. The sprinting thing stops instantly. It looks up. You can’t see its eyes clearly—but you feel it seeing you anyway.
Garage door to the house is locked. You open it slowly. Your car sits there like a loyal dog. Also: tools. Also: an exit to the driveway.

choices.label: Try the car keys and prep to leave
Crack the garage door a little and look outside

nodes.text: You grab a heavy tool. It’s not a sword, but it’ll do. Your arms feel slightly more confident than your soul.

choices.label: Look outside from the garage door crack
Go back inside and lock down

nodes.text: You lift the garage door two inches. The driveway is empty. But the street… there’s movement near the neighbor’s mailbox. Too twitchy. Too wrong.

choices.label: Close it and leave in the car anyway
Sneak out on foot through the backyard gate

nodes.text: You peek again. The porch is empty now. But there’s a wet smear on the glass. And you don’t remember it being there before.

choices.label: Go to the garage and leave

nodes.text: You shove a chair and a small table into place. The door bucks once. Twice. The wood groans. You have seconds, not minutes.

choices.label: Sprint to the garage for the car

nodes.text: You grip the knife. The door cracks open a finger-width. A hand snakes through—fingernails broken, skin gray. It grabs for you.

choices.label: Stab the hand and slam the door

nodes.text: You stab. It shrieks like a broken radio. The hand retreats. The door slams. Your hands shake. You’re alive. For now.

choices.label: Run to the garage and leave

nodes.text: You hide in a closet. It smells like detergent and denial. Outside, you hear soft footsteps… then a scrape along the wall, like someone searching with their hands.
Upstairs closet. The house creaks. Then you hear it—your downstairs window cracking. Something is inside your house now. Congrats.

choices.label: Try to sneak down the stairs
Stay hidden and hope it passes

nodes.text: You step onto the top stair. It creaks. You freeze. Downstairs, a head tilts. You hear wet breathing.

choices.label: Commit. Move fast to the garage door

nodes.text: You shove a dresser against the bedroom door. You look at the window. Roof outside. You could climb out and drop into the backyard… maybe.
Cold air hits your face. You climb onto the roof. Below, the backyard is dark. The fence gate is slightly open. Like someone already went through.

choices.label: Drop into the backyard and run
Try to reach the driveway from the side roofline

nodes.text: You edge along the roofline. One wrong step and you’re a news headline. You see your car in the driveway… and movement near it.

choices.label: Drop near the car and run for it

nodes.text: You reach the garage door from inside the house. Something shuffles behind you in the dark living room. You don’t look back.

choices.label: Enter the garage and close the door fast

nodes.text: A hand clamps your shoulder. It’s cold. Too strong. You twist away but it rakes your arm—skin breaks. You feel a sting.

choices.label: Break free and run into the garage

nodes.text: Backyard. Damp grass. The fence gate is open. A neighbor’s porch light flickers across the yard. You hear something crawling under a bush nearby.

choices.label: Sneak through the gate to the street

nodes.text: You slide into the driver’s seat. Hands shaking. The keys are—where are the keys? You fumble. Outside, you hear a wet thud against the garage door.

choices.label: Get out and escape on foot (through the back)

nodes.text: You click the flashlight. You spot your keys in the cup holder. You also spot a smear of blood on the passenger window—fresh.
The engine turns over. Loud. Too loud. Outside, something slams the garage door again. You hit the button to open it. The door rises… slowly… like it hates you.

choices.label: Gun it the moment there’s space

nodes.text: You drop near the car. Something shambles out from between two vehicles—its head jerks toward you. It starts sprinting. Not limping. Sprinting.

choices.label: Dive into the car and start it

nodes.text: You gun it. The moment the garage clears your hood, a figure lunges from the side. It clips the car and rolls away like a ragdoll. You don’t stop. You keep going.

choices.label: Head toward the main road (more danger, faster escape)

nodes.text: You wait. The garage door opens higher. In that extra second, something squeezes under and reaches for your hood—then it stands upright.
You sneak through yards and shadows. The street is a horror museum: open doors, abandoned cars, someone’s bicycle tossed in a ditch. You hear distant screaming—then silence.

choices.label: Head to the neighborhood park (open space)
Go house-to-house looking for a safe light

nodes.text: You run. Feet pounding. Your breathing is a siren. Behind you, something answers with fast footsteps. You’re not alone out here.

choices.label: Cut into a random house’s backyard

nodes.text: You cut into a backyard and hop a small fence. A dog bowl sits on the porch. No dog. You hear the chasing footsteps slow down—like it lost you.
You hide behind the shed. You hear sniffing. Then a low, wet growl. Something is on the other side of the wood panel—way too close.

choices.label: Fight it off with your tool

nodes.text: You swing the tool blindly as it lunges around the shed corner. You connect. It drops. Not dead… but slowed. Your arms burn.
You approach a house with a faint light inside. Curtains drawn. You hear someone whispering. This could be help… or a trap.
You knock. A voice: 'Who is it?' They sound terrified… but human. The door chain rattles.

choices.label: Tell the truth: 'I’m running from infected. I need shelter.'

nodes.text: Silence. Then: 'Nice try.' The light goes off. You hear a heavy piece of furniture scraping against the door from inside. You’ve been rejected professionally.
The chain opens an inch. A person peers out. They see your hands—empty (or not). They whisper: 'Get inside, fast.'

choices.label: Decline. You need to find someone else.

nodes.text: You reach the neighborhood park. Open field. Playground. A gazebo. The openness is safer… until it isn’t. You see shapes moving near the swings.

choices.label: Creep around the edges toward the gazebo
Risk it—sprint across to the far street

nodes.text: You sprint across the park. The shapes turn. Heads snap. You realize they weren’t slow. They were waiting.
Under the gazebo, you find a stash: a backpack, a battery radio, and a note: 'IF YOU’RE READING THIS, DON’T TRUST THE MAIN ROAD.'

choices.label: Leave the park via side streets

nodes.text: Backpack secured. You feel like a protagonist now. Which is… not always a good thing.
Static. Then a shaky voice: '…evacuation at the high school gym… avoid main roads… if you’re bitten—' The message cuts out.

choices.label: Ignore evac. Find a safehouse instead

nodes.text: You take the main road. Bad idea vibes. Abandoned cars clog the lanes. Far ahead, you see flashing lights… and silhouettes moving between them.

choices.label: Turn around and take side streets

nodes.text: You push forward. Something slams your passenger window from outside. Another hit. Your car swerves. You’re about to lose control.
Side streets. Quieter. Darker. You see a couple survivors up ahead near a cul-de-sac. They’re armed. They’re watching you.
A survivor calls out: 'Stop right there. You bitten?' Their flashlight beam shakes. They’re scared—same as you.

choices.label: Tell the truth: show your arms

nodes.text: They narrow their eyes. One raises their weapon slightly. 'Everybody says that.'

choices.label: Admit it: 'Okay, I’m scared. I don’t know.'

nodes.text: They check you quickly. If you have a visible scratch, their whole mood changes.

choices.label: If you're scratched, beg them to let you come anyway
If you're NOT scratched, join them

nodes.text: They see the scratch. One whispers, 'No. No way.' Another looks guilty. They’re deciding if you’re a person or a future problem.
You have a direction now: the high school gym might be evac. Or you can hole up somewhere and pray morning comes.

choices.label: Head toward the high school gym (evac rumor)
Find/return to a safehouse (survivors / a lit house)

nodes.text: You reach a safehouse scenario—either you’re let in by survivors, or you find a home that’s secured. For now, you’re breathing. For now.

end.title: Act 1 Complete: Shelter Found
end.reason: You survived the night by staying quiet, moving smart, and getting under a roof with real barriers.

nodes.text: You move toward the high school. In the distance you see floodlights and hear a generator. The night isn’t over… but you made it to the next chapter.

end.title: Act 1 Complete: Evac Route
end.reason: You escaped the neighborhood and set a goal. Act 2 will decide if evac is real… or a trap.

nodes.text: The night wins. Everything goes loud, then quiet. You don’t get a second try out here.

end.title: You Died
end.reason: Tonight punished hesitation, noise, and bad timing.

nodes.text: You feel it later. The heat under your skin. The itch in your veins. You keep moving—because stopping would mean admitting what you already know.

end.title: You’re Infected
end.reason: A scratch is enough. Act 2 might still be playable… but the clock is now inside you.

nodes.text: You wait in the dark. Minutes feel like hours. Somewhere outside, glass breaks. Somewhere closer, something drags itself along your wall.

choices.label: You can’t wait—move to the garage

nodes.text: Time passes. The house makes a sound like it’s settling… but it’s not the house. Something is inside now, moving slow, learning the layout.
You wait behind a barricaded door. The scratching starts. Then the knob turns. Then the door pushes inward like it’s being hugged by a truck.
